headline: AI coding just got agentic—Windsurf's Wave 13 dominates, Claude gets 1M context, and agents finally act like teammates

picks.name: Windsurf
picks.tagline: AI-native IDE with Cascade agent for autonomous multi-file coding
picks.summary: Windsurf hit #1 this week with Wave 13 dropping sick autonomous agent abilities. Write, test, debug, deploy—all without manually approving every diff. It's like having a dev partner instead of an autocomplete.
picks.tryIt: Free tier with 25 credits/month, Pro at $15/month with 500 credits. Go risk-free.
picks.bestFor: Devs who want agentic workflows without leaving their IDE

picks.name: Claude Sonnet 4.6
picks.tagline: 1M context window, better computer use, power-user pricing at $3/$15
picks.summary: Anthropic finally shipped 1M token context in GA. It's lowkey insane for handling huge codebases without paying Opus rates. Computer use improvements mean Claude can actually drive UI tasks better now.
picks.tryIt: Free on claude.ai, paid users get the upgrades. Already live.
picks.bestFor: Builders who work with massive projects, coders needing long-context reasoning

picks.name: Perplexity Personal Computer
picks.tagline: Your Mac becomes an autonomous local AI agent (waitlist live)
picks.summary: Perplexity's new system lets your spare Mac run as a local AI agent handling research, automation, and tasks in the background. Basically turns any machine into your personal AI assistant without cloud dependency.
picks.tryIt: Waitlist is open now. Get early access before it launches.
picks.bestFor: Anyone wanting local AI agents that respect privacy

picks.name: Copilot Cowork
picks.tagline: Microsoft's agentic copilot that actually executes tasks across Outlook, Teams, Excel
picks.summary: Stop describing what you want and waiting—Cowork builds the plan AND executes it. Reschedule your Tuesday, pull a client deck from scattered emails, done. Claude powers the engine underneath.
picks.tryIt: Rolling out late March 2026. Keep an eye on your Microsoft 365 account.
picks.bestFor: Teams drowning in context-switching between Outlook, Teams, and spreadsheets

watchNext.title: Gemini 3.1 Pro enters coding models at #2 with best price-to-performance in frontier tier ($2/$12 pricing)
watchNext.why: 77.1% on ARC-AGI-2 (more than doubles 3.0's reasoning), 24-language voice, full video processing. This is the quiet threat to Claude's dominance—same capability, cheaper execution.

watchNext.title: Anthropic Claude add-ins now live in Excel/PowerPoint on Mac & Windows (paid plans only)
watchNext.why: fr this marks serious enterprise integration. Claude sitting inside Excel/PowerPoint means productivity workflows finally got a brain—not just prompt-pasting in a new tab.
